_comment: Stryker mutation testing — scoped to the DSL semantic core (resolver + analyzer). Mutation testing complements line/branch coverage: it proves the test *assertions* actually catch regressions, not merely that lines were executed. Kept narrow (two files) so a full run finishes in minutes and gates the highest-risk modules.
_ignoreStatic_comment: Skip static mutants (module-load-time code). Stryker flagged 211 such mutants (~14% of total) consuming ~52% of run time because every static mutant forces a full re-import of the module under every test. They mostly live in top-level constant tables (keyword maps, alias sets) that are validated indirectly by the parser/lexer suites anyway. Ignoring them keeps a full mutation run to a few minutes while still measuring assertion strength on the actual *logic* branches.
_thresholds_comment: Mutation score is a SEPARATE, stricter signal than line/branch coverage: it measures whether assertions actually catch regressions. The first honest scoped baseline (resolver+analyzer, ignoreStatic) is 49.72% (analyzer 62% covered-only, resolver 49% covered-only). `break` is set to 45 — just BELOW the measured baseline — so CI fails only on a *regression* (assertions deleted/weakened), not on this known starting point. Raise `break` toward `low`/`high` as survived mutants (mostly ConditionalExpression + StringLiteral diagnostic text) are killed by strengthening assertions on error message/suggestion content.
